draft.introduction: John 14:1–31 opens the Farewell Discourse, where Jesus promises to prepare a place for his disciples and declares “I am the way, the truth, and the life.” It also introduces the Paraclete, the Spirit of truth.

translation.text: Let not your hearts be troubled. Believe in God; believe also in me. In my Father’s house are many rooms. If it were not so, would I have told you that I go to prepare a place for you? I am the way, and the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.
translation.original_text: μὴ ταρασσέσθω ὑμῶν ἡ καρδία · πιστεύετε εἰς τὸν θεόν καὶ εἰς ἐμὲ πιστεύετε

translator_notes.note: The triple predicate “way, truth, life” (ἡ ὁδὸς καὶ ἡ ἀλήθεια καὶ ἡ ζωή) is the sixth “I am” saying. The exclusivist claim “no one comes to the Father except through me” has been among the most debated verses in interfaith dialogue.
